meaning: n.严格,严厉,苛刻,严密,严酷,精确
examples: Schools acknowledge the rigor of European secondary training, and will give up to a year's credit to foreigners who have passed their high school exams.
Some US schools acknowledge the rigor of European secondary training, and will give up to a year's credit to foreigners who have passed their high school exams.
Science has to have some rigor.
But what if the cumulative lack of rigor, of critical self-deprecation, turns into systemic publication bias?
They needed the same analytical rigor applied to measuring impact as for measuring returns.
